label: Persistence Trap Detector Pattern
description: Detects preserved artifacts whose executable authority has diverged from current legitimacy conditions.

nodes.label: Persistence Trap Detector Pattern domain
nodes.description: Domain where temporal authority, revocation state, and enforcement evidence are evaluated.

nodes.label: Source authority
nodes.description: Authority that issued, delegated, revoked, or preserved the governing claim.

nodes.label: Subject artifact
nodes.description: Artifact, delegation, license, credential, or decision record being evaluated.

nodes.label: Temporal governance policy
nodes.description: Defines validity windows, cache bounds, propagation expectations, and execution preconditions.

nodes.label: Revocation event
nodes.description: Revocation or authority-change event whose effect must propagate to execution environments.

nodes.label: Temporal verifier
nodes.description: Verifier that checks current authority state at action time.

nodes.label: Execution decision
nodes.description: Allow, deny, quarantine, or escalate decision based on current authority state.

nodes.label: Temporal governance receipt
nodes.description: Receipt capturing authority state, revocation freshness, propagation evidence, and decision outcome.

nodes.label: Enforcement effect
nodes.description: Runtime enforcement outcome applied to the artifact or action.

nodes.label: Execution node
nodes.description: Local relying environment where stale or current authority is accepted or rejected.

edges.description: Authority binds the artifact to an admissible scope and lifecycle.
Temporal policy constrains whether the artifact can still be used.
Revocation changes the executable state of the artifact.
Verifier checks current authority and revocation state at execution time.
Execution node supplies observed local enforcement and cache state.
Temporal verifier informs the decision outcome.
Decision produces an enforcement or quarantine effect.
